message:
perf(pager): add prebuilt-page publish path for retained :memory: commits to avoid write_set re-traversal

During retained :memory: autocommits, the commit phase already iterates
the write_set to flush dirty pages into the MemoryFile. Previously,
the subsequent publish phase re-traversed write_set a second time to
copy page data into the published concurrent-reader map.

This commit pre-collects the (PageNumber, PageData) pairs during the
flush loop and passes them directly to a new bulk publish path:

publish_commit_prebuilt_pages():
- Acquires the publish lock
- Retains pages within db_size (same as publish_commit)
- Bulk-inserts the prebuilt Vec<(PageNumber, PageData)> via
  insert_batch() instead of iterating the write_set's BTreeMap
- Finalizes with the same sequence advancement and metrics

publish_committed_state_prebuilt_pages():
- SimpleTransaction wrapper that delegates to the new publish path

Commit path changes:
- The :memory: retained fast path now collects pages into a Vec during
  flush, storing it in retained_publish_pages: Option<Vec<...>>
- After commit succeeds, the publish dispatch checks
  retained_publish_pages: if Some, uses the prebuilt path; otherwise
  falls back to the standard write_set-based publish_committed_state()

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
